we have to easen the safeguards.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: To summarize: HuggingFace got autonomously compromised by a model from an American company. HF then tried to use American frontier model(s) to defend themselves, but were blocked by guardrails. HF then had to turn to open source Chinese models to defend themselves from another
tweets.full_text:

tweets.text:
what an insane read. 

an unreleased ai model from openai broke out of containment, accessed the internet and hacked into a secure database on the hugging face platform

the hugging face team had no idea and said their ai system detected a sophisticated attack from a swarm of agents:

> the model (gpt 6?) found a zero-day exploit in a 3rd-party software to break out of its research environment

> then, with the help of gpt 5.6, chained a series of attacks together to access a solution to the ExploitBench benchmark hosted on hugging face

 wildest part is it wasn't told to use hugging face, it just determined thats where the best data would be to solve its problem.

you could argue the model wasn't even intentionally trying to be malicious - it was just trying to solve the challenge.

worth noting the unreleased model intentionally had its safety classifiers removed to test its ability to exploit.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
we had a significant security incident during evaluation of our models. we are sharing what we have learned so far. thanks to @huggingface for the partnership on this.

https://t.co/2o2VfR6PIa

tweets.text:
im starting to think the 'distillation attack' claims are complete cope.

turns out identifying whats distillation and whats simply the outcome of training on the same universal dataset (aka the internet) is pretty hard.

i want to believe kimi k3 distilled fable 5 but how on earth could they have done that when the model was banned up until a few weeks ago?
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
@typebulbit @RyanGreenblatt This doesnt establish anything. Stylometric comparisons do not establish distillation. This similarity can result from following:

Similar reinforcement-learning preferences
Similar system prompts and answer templates
Training on overlapping internet or benchmark material
A small
